reasoning: SPY at 663.72, overnight gap-down 2.34 points. Premarket rallied to 665.38 (Active resistance) but failed to hold above. Price now sandwiched between premarket low 663.09 (63¢ support) and premarket high 665.38 (166¢ resistance). Multiple stacked resistances above (665.38 → 666.65 Carry → 667.87 pivot R1) with compressed support below suggests asymmetric downside risk if 665.38 fails. MIXED ribbon (39¢ spread) shows indecision, no bullish confirmation. Rising VIX (3.07%) in gap-down context elevates uncertainty. Battle level is 665.38 — if it holds, expect test of 663.09 support; if broken, upside extends to 666.65 Carry level.

primary.condition: if SPY fails at 665.38 and holds 663.09 support

secondary.condition: if SPY breaks above 665.38 on RTH open

key_observations: Overnight gap-down 2.34 (down 0.35%) sets negative opening tone
Premarket high 665.38 is active resistance — immediate battle zone for RTH open in ~2 hours
Support at 663.09 is extremely fresh (premarket low, 15 min old) and very tight (63¢ below current price)
MIXED ribbon (39¢ spread, Fast 664.43 > Slow 664.07) with no clear stack direction = indecision, not a bullish signal
Stacked resistances above (665.38 → 666.65 Carry → 667.87 pivot R1) with compressed support below creates asymmetric downside bias
Rising VIX (+3.07% to 16.78) in a gap-down market = elevated volatility risk, favor directional move over range play
